query:
INSERT INTO quiz_questions (lessonId, question, questionType, options, correctAnswer, questionOrder) VALUES
(48, 'What is the central theme of Paul''s Epistle to the Ephesians?', 'multiple_choice', '["Individual salvation only", "God''s eternal purpose to unite all things in Christ and build His church as a display of His wisdom", "Moral improvement through self-effort", "Escape from the material world"]', 'God''s eternal purpose to unite all things in Christ and build His church as a display of His wisdom', 1),
(48, 'How does Philippians demonstrate Paul''s perspective on suffering and joy in Christian ministry?', 'multiple_choice', '["Suffering should be avoided at all costs", "Joy in Christ transcends circumstances, and suffering can advance the gospel and deepen fellowship with Christ", "Christians should never experience joy during suffering", "Suffering indicates God''s displeasure"]', 'Joy in Christ transcends circumstances, and suffering can advance the gospel and deepen fellowship with Christ', 2),
(48, 'What is the primary theological concern Paul addresses in Colossians?', 'multiple_choice', '["Financial stewardship", "The supremacy and sufficiency of Christ against false teaching that diminishes His person and work", "Church government structures", "End times prophecy"]', 'The supremacy and sufficiency of Christ against false teaching that diminishes His person and work', 3),
(48, 'True or False: Paul''s letter to Philemon demonstrates that the gospel transforms social relationships while working within existing structures toward ultimate redemption.', 'true_false', '["True", "False"]', 'True', 4),
(48, 'In 2-3 sentences, explain the theological significance of the Christ hymn in Philippians 2:5-11.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'The Christ hymn presents Jesus'' incarnation, humiliation, and exaltation as the pattern for Christian humility and service, demonstrating that true greatness comes through self-giving love (Philippians 2:5-8). It affirms both Christ''s pre-existent deity and His genuine humanity, showing how He emptied Himself of divine privileges while retaining His divine nature. God''s exaltation of the crucified Christ to universal lordship reveals that the way of the cross is the path to glory, both for Christ and His followers.', 5),
(49, 'What is the primary purpose of the Pastoral Epistles (1-2 Timothy, Titus)?', 'multiple_choice', '["To provide systematic theology", "To give practical instruction for church leadership, sound doctrine, and ministry organization", "To address persecution from Rome", "To explain end times prophecy"]', 'To give practical instruction for church leadership, sound doctrine, and ministry organization', 1),
(49, 'How does Hebrews present Jesus Christ in relation to the Old Testament sacrificial system?', 'multiple_choice', '["Jesus is one of many acceptable sacrifices", "Jesus is the superior and final high priest who offered the perfect sacrifice once for all", "The Old Testament system is still necessary alongside Christ", "Jesus merely reformed the existing system"]', 'Jesus is the superior and final high priest who offered the perfect sacrifice once for all', 2),
(49, 'What is the theological significance of the "better" theme throughout Hebrews?', 'multiple_choice', '["It denigrates the Old Testament as worthless", "It shows Christ''s superiority to angels, Moses, the priesthood, and the old covenant, fulfilling what they foreshadowed", "It promotes Jewish legalism", "It has no practical application"]', 'It shows Christ''s superiority to angels, Moses, the priesthood, and the old covenant, fulfilling what they foreshadowed', 3),
(49, 'True or False: The Pastoral Epistles emphasize that sound doctrine and godly character are both essential for church leadership.', 'true_false', '["True", "False"]', 'True', 4),
(49, 'In 2-3 sentences, explain how Hebrews 11 defines faith and demonstrates its role in the life of God''s people.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'Hebrews 11:1 defines faith as confidence in what we hope for and assurance about what we cannot see, grounding Christian hope in God''s promises rather than present circumstances. The chapter presents a catalog of Old Testament saints who lived by faith, demonstrating that faith has always been the principle by which God''s people relate to Him and persevere through trials. This "great cloud of witnesses" encourages believers to run with endurance, fixing their eyes on Jesus, the ultimate example and object of faith (Hebrews 12:1-2).', 5)
command:
mysql --batch --raw --column-names --default-character-set=utf8mb4 --host gateway02.us-east-1.prod.aws.tidbcloud.com --port 4000 --user 2KiCrCqNDFcXt7P.577d1a63eeb5 --database P2Dpw9MQsmxzdUQCfGjV6b --execute INSERT INTO quiz_questions (lessonId, question, questionType, options, correctAnswer, questionOrder) VALUES

This "great cloud of witnesses" encourages believers to run with endurance, fixing their eyes on Jesus, the ultimate example and object of faith (Hebrews 12:1-2).', 5)
